title: Ellipsis Sum Function
prompt_md:
## Goal
Write a program using an ellipsis function to compute the sum of N integers passed as variadic arguments.

## Input
- Line 1: integer N (1 <= N <= 5)
- Lines 2..N+1: one integer per line

## Output
Print exactly one line: the sum of the N integers.

## Requirements
1. Implement `int sumN(int count, ...)` using `va_list`, `va_start`, `va_arg`, and `va_end`.
2. `main` reads all N values into local variables and calls `sumN` passing each as a separate argument.
3. Print the result on a single line.
4. For simplicity, read up to 5 values from stdin; for N < 5, only pass N values to `sumN`.

## Notes
- Include `<cstdarg>` for the va_* macros.
- This exercise demonstrates the mechanics of ellipsis; in real code, prefer `std::initializer_list`.

title: Ellipsis Count Bug
prompt_md:
## Goal
Find and fix the bug in this program that uses an ellipsis function to find the maximum of N integers.

## Input
- Line 1: integer N (1 <= N <= 5)
- Lines 2..N+1: one integer per line

## Output
Print exactly one line: the maximum of the N integers.

## Requirements
1. The bug causes the function to read the wrong number of values from the variadic list.
2. Fixing the bug is a one-character or one-number change.
3. All five dispatch branches in `main` are correct and should not be changed.
